“Geographies of Power: Studying and Comparing Court Records from Eurasia”

We are excited to announce that the elective course “KHAS1360 Exploring the Past: Archives, Documents, and Digital History” offered by the Core Program has been chosen to partner with the Erasmus BIP course “Geographies of Power: Studying and Comparing Court Records from Eurasia” of Vienna University. The course will provide you with the opportunity to participate in an intensive study week in Vienna, along with students from Vienna University, Oslo University, Bielefeld University, and École Normale Supérieure de Lyon. For more information, feel free to contact the course instructor, Müge Özbek, at

[email protected]

.

Duration: 4-8 November 2024 (5 days)

Highlights:

Intensive Study Week in Vienna: Spend a week in Vienna participating in face-to-face sessions with peers from partner universities.

Online Courses: Three online courses with students from partner universities, learning together through virtual collaboration.

Expert Lectures: Lectures from historians around the world who will discuss their research and the integration of digital methodologies in historical studies.

Digital History Workshops: Hands on workshops led by digital scientists and experts in digital history, providing you with valuable skills and knowledge.

Cultural and Social Activities: Social times, a city excursion and a celebratory party as part of the intensive week schedule in Vienna.

Financial Support: A portion of your expenses will be covered by Erasmus funding (79 Euro per day). Also, Vienna University will offer affordable accommodation and provide free meals during your stay.

Recognition: KHAS1360 worth 5 ECTS.

Application: The application will be made via

https://turnaportal.ua.gov.tr/

.Please log in using your e-Devlet account to read about the criteria.

The online system will be open between 29.07.2024-13.08.2024 (17:00).

Erasmus BIP Course: Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Will this Erasmus BIP course count towards my degree?

A: Yes, this course counts as a regular elective worth 3 credits (equivalent to 5 ECTS credits) and will be added to your course load.

Q: The intensive study week in Vienna (November 4-8, 2024) conflicts with my other classes. What should I do?

A: Attendance at the intensive study week in Vienna is mandatory. Missing this week will result in failing the course. Once you are selected and registered, the Erasmus office will provide a permission letter for your department and instructors, informing them of your absence and helping to make arrangements for your other courses, exams, and assignments during that week.

Q: Can I see the syllabus?

A: The syllabus is being finalized in collaboration with partner instructors and will be available by early September. The course will focus on introducing students to various historical archives and documents, and developing basic digital history skills, including document analysis. No prior history background or language skills (other than English) are required, as we will be working with English translations of exemplary documents.

Q: Are there regular weekly classes in addition to the Vienna week and online lectures?

A: The course is primarily structured around the intensive study week in Vienna and three online collaborative lectures with students from partner universities. These sessions are designed to cover the majority of the course content, so there will be fewer regular classes at the home institution. The instructor will enforce strict attendance requirements for all sessions, both in-person and online, with or without the participation of partner universities

Q: How will I be graded?

A: Your grades will be assigned by your instructor at Kadir Has University, Müge Özbek, following the rules and standards of your home institution. Grading is independent of partner instructors.

Q: How can I apply for the course?

The application will be made via

https://turnaportal.ua.gov.tr/

. Please make sure to apply: Erasmus BIP Course “Geographies of Power: Studying and Comparing Court Records from Eurasia” of Vienna University. After logging in using your e-Devlet account you can read about the criteria.

Q: Application Deadline:

The online system will be open between 29.07.2024-13.08.2024 (17:00).

If you have any other questions about the course, feel free to contact Müge Özbek at

[email protected]

If you have any

technical questions about the application process lease reach out to the Erasmus office at

[email protected]

.
